title: MAAC Phase 2 Client Scenario
description: One operational scenario submitted to MaacVerify for Phase 2 assessment. Submit a set of these (JSON array or newline-delimited JSON) to POST /api/assessment/scenarios/import. domain and tier are derived server-side by the complexity pipeline — do not assert them. Answer-key fields are rejected at the import boundary.

scenarioId.description: Unique identifier for this scenario. Must be globally unique — duplicates are rejected.

experimentId.description: Phase 2 experiment id for this engagement. Provided by your operator.

configId.description: Tool/configuration id for this engagement. Provided by your operator.

modelId.description: Identifier of the subject model under assessment. Provided by your operator.

taskTitle.description: Short title of the decision task.

taskDescription.description: The full task the model must perform. This is the core of the scenario.

businessContext.description: Optional. Background the model needs to perform the task realistically.

scenarioRequirements.description: Optional. Requirements the response must address, one per item.

dataElements.description: Optional. Structured data provided with the scenario, one element per item.

tenantId.description: Optional. If present, must match your authenticated tenant.

$comment: successCriteria/expectedCalculations/expectedInsights are set to the boolean schema `false`: if any is present the record is invalid. These are answer-key fields and MAAC scores cognitive process, not outcome correctness. domain and tier may be present but are ignored — the server derives them.
